item.title: Guild Wars 3 reveal speculation ramps up a notch as domain digging unearths a recently updated website
item.content:encoded:
Following a mysterious teaser from ArenaNet earlier this week

Chatter about a potential Guild Wars 3 reveal at today's Summer Game Fest has picked up following the discovery that a website domain that'd seem to fit the MMORPG and appears to be registered to NC Corporation has been updated today, June 5th. This follows teasers developers ArenaNet have been posting to Guild Wars 2's social media accounts this week, pointing to something Guild Warsy going down at SGF.

"The wind stirs. The world shifts. Stand ready," read the caption of the teaser video from the studio which initially stoked the Guild Wars 3 speculation, especially when ArenaNet didn't quickly move to shut it down.

That brings us to today and a Guild Wars vtuber going by Lucy posting that upon looking up the website domain guildwars3.com, they found it'd been updated today. According to the vtuber, the domain "now points to awsdns, which are web servers of Amazon Web Services" and the domain of Guild Wars 2's website (guildwars2.com) "is also parked on same name servers".

The Guild Wars 3 (https://t.co/rsa2zPffNo) domain has been updated just now. It now points to awsdns, which are web servers of Amazon Web Services

For comparision Guild Wars 2 domain is also parked on same name servers. WE ARE GETTING GUILD WARS 3 pic.twitter.com/luNyWlg5ot

— lucy ✨💜 (@lucynavt) June 5, 2026

To see this content please enable targeting cookies.

I've looked up both domain through the same lookup site Lucy appears to have used, whois.com, to verify their screenshots were genuine. The Guild Wars 3 site has indeed been updated on June 5th 2026, points to AWSDNS servers as Guild Wars 2's site does, and is listed as being registered to an NCsoft Corp whose address looks to match recently renamed Guild Wars publishers NC Corporation's Korean HQ.

I even went a step further and checked the whois listing for NC Corporation's corporate site nc.com, and found the registrant info to match. So, it certainly appears NC have prepped for a Guild Wars 3 site whenever they need one. Whether the recent update indicates it'll be revealed during tonight's showcase is another matter. We'll just have to see.

In the mean time, read our Guild Wars 2 review.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/guild-wars-3-reveal-speculation-ramps-up-a-notch-as-domain-digging-unearths-a-recently-updated-website
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Is Microsoft's $69 billion Activision Blizzard deal paying off? Xbox boss Asha Sharma says it's "hard to say how to think about those decisions"
item.content:encoded:
"It was bought at a time before ChatGPT"

Just under 1000 days ago, or about two and a half years ago if you measure time like a normal person, Microsoft completed their acquisition of Activision Blizzard following a lengthy legal process. It cost $68.7 billion and meant Xbox's parent company now lorded over the likes of Call of Duty, World of Warcraft, and Overwatch. Not a small thing for the corporate behemoth to have done, then. Looking back in 2026, was it a shrewd move? Well, amid some bigging up of the series I just mentioned, current Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has said it's "hard to say how to think about those decisions".

This answer came during an interview with Bloomberg Tech. The host pointed out that Sharma "inherited" the deal, which has been followed by layoffs, cancellations, and a Game Pass price hike, as part of asking the exec whether she believes the acquisition is paying off.

Sharma's response started how you might expect. "I don't know anybody in entertainment who wouldn't want Call of Duty, which is now grossing in more revenue than the Marvel Cinematic Universe," she said. "Who wouldn't want one of the top three apps in the world with Candy Crush? Who wouldn't want World of Warcraft? And who wouldn't want a team that for 20 years has been able to create predictable hits every single year? I love Activision Blizzard King."

Then, things got a lot more intriguing. "Look, it was bought at a time before ChatGPT," the exec continued. "It was bought at a time when our strategy was predominantly on the core console. It was at a time when we were right in the middle of Covid. So, hard to say how to think about those decisions. But I think these are incredible assets and we intend to continue to invest in them."

On one hand, the ambivalent shrugging that creeps in midway through the second half of that answer could well just be Sharma making an effort to further distance herself from the decisions of the Phil Spencer/Sarah Bond regime she replaced, as she's seemed very keen to do since gaining the big green throne. On the other, to have hints of potential buyer's remorse creeping into the boss of a company's on-stage answers about a deal as huge as the Activision Blizzard one just a few years down the line struck me as fairly shocking.

Even if it's abundantly clear at this juncture whether such a move has played out how the company planned - Spencer and Bond's departures signify a firm no - I'd expect their bosses to still be in the 'say nice things and try to make it work' stage. That's firmly where Sharma is for the rest of her answer, but to even momentarily let the mask slip feels noteworthy, if not out of out of character for an exec who's spent some of her early reign apologising to vocal online Xbox fans about moves the console makers have made.

CEOs being a bit more open and honest about big decisions like this, as Sharma's shown thus far, is a double-edged sword. It can help them convince us regular peons that they're not living in some corporate dream land without a finger anywhere near the pulse, but not defaulting to unflinchingly defending the company line can also result in situations like this where the seeds of doubt in terms of your commitment to certain things can start to creep in. That way lies wishy-washy ditheriness, arguably the most damaging impression someone in Sharma's position can give.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/is-microsofts-69-billion-activision-blizzard-deal-paying-off-xbox-boss-asha-sharma-says-its-hard-to-say-how-to-think-about-those-decisions
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Summer Game Fest 2026 livestream: Join our liveblog for all the main show and Day of the Devs announcements
item.content:encoded:
A double bill of ferociously typed updates

Live

Our annual Summer Game Fest liveblog returns this evening, June 5th, for another up-to-the-minute updatening of all the news, trailers, and announcements unveiled during the Summer of Geoff’s main showcase. And, since it follows straight after, we’re sticking around for the indie-focused Day of the Devs. That’s - hang on - two liveblogs in one. Unprecedented, except for those times we did it before.

What awaits us within these three-odd hours? Game adverts, obvs. We know, or are at least arrogantly certain, that Respawn’s XCOM-like Star Wars: Zero Company will make an appearance at the headline showcase, as will open-world racer Clutch, a new Soulframe trailer, and something Among Us-shaped. Gazing into the rumour abyss, there’s a possibility of updates on The Witcher 4 and/or upcoming Witcher 3 expansion Songs of the Past, maybe a little Resident Evil: Code Veronica remake, or perhaps some cheeky Persona 6? We’ll see. Marvel 1943: Rise of Hydra and a new Injustice fighting game have had their whispers too.

As for Day of the Devs, confirmed games include Sunset Visitor’s Prove You’re Human, Lovecraftian horror-fisher Dreadmoor, squash 'n' stretch action roguelike Into the Unwell, and environmentally-minded road trip RPG Ithaca. N++ devs Metanet Software will also be revealing something new.

In short: there will be games at Summer Game Fest. You can join us for all of them as we watch the livestream (we’ll specifically be following the official stream, embedded here) and share thoughts in the comment-friendly liveblog tool below. The primary SGF showcase begins at 10pm BST / 5pm EDT, but we’ll be settling down around 9pm BST / 4pm PDT, both to catch any potential pre-show and for me to warm up my postin' fingers. In the meantime, we’ll be popping in occasionally to share any early-onset leaks and news from throughout the day. See you soon.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/sgf-2026-livestream
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
